responseMessageContents.text: The native app code is in the `expo/` folder and synced to their GitHub repo, but this workspace is locked to Swift so I can only show the Swift placeholder here—the actual React Native app needs to be run from their GitHub repository.

Your native app is safe — here's exactly where it lives:

**1. In this workspace** — the full codebase is in the `expo/` folder: 100+ screens (`app/`), admin suite (67 files), IVX owner AI modules, chat, landing page (`ivxholding-landing/`), deploy scripts — all of it. I just verified it's all still there.

**2. In your GitHub repo** — every change is auto-synced to `ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest` (main branch). That's your source of truth, and Render/your other tooling can deploy from it.

**Why you don't see it in the preview here:** this Rork project was created as an **iOS/Swift** project — that choice is locked at creation. The preview window here can only run the Swift app (`ios/`, currently a placeholder). The React Native app cannot be registered or previewed in this workspace — I've retried it; it's a hard platform restriction.

**To see your native app running:**
- Open your **other Rork project** (the one created as React Native/Expo) — that's where the preview works, or
- Create a new Rork project selecting **React Native**, and it will pull straight from your GitHub repo

Nothing was lost or deleted — the code exists in both places. Want me to keep working on the code itself (fixes, features, audits) from here? That works fine — only the live preview doesn't.
